tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated serum creatinine and proteinuria, suggesting possible renal impairment. Urine is cloudy, brown, with low specific gravity, glucosuria, ketonuria, and bilirubinuria, but no hematuria or leukocyturia. HbA1c is low, making chronic hyperglycemia unlikely. Findings are consistent with acute or chronic kidney dysfunction; further nephrological evaluation is recommended. No clear evidence of urinary tract infection.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
